finding: A resolvable hostname alone does not prove which repository commit is served. A well-known manifest stamped from GITHUB_SHA and GITHUB_REPOSITORY creates a deploy-time identity binding that can be fetched over HTTPS and compared with the workflow source commit.
implementation_handoff: Run node cloudflare-static/stamp-deployment-manifest.mjs cloudflare-static after checkout and before wrangler pages deploy.
Add node --test cloudflare-static/stamp-deployment-manifest.test.mjs to the workflow contract suite.
After deployment, fetch the returned deployment URL plus /.well-known/mirror-cartographer-research.json and require source_commit to equal GITHUB_SHA.
falsification_route: Deploy a commit with the stamper wired into the workflow, fetch the well-known manifest from the returned Cloudflare URL, and compare its source_commit to the workflow GITHUB_SHA. Reject the proof if the file is absent, malformed, cached from another commit, or mismatched.
limits: The module is committed but not yet wired into the deployment workflow.
The deterministic tests were committed but could not be executed through the connector in this run.
Cloudflare credentials and a successful deployment remain externally required.
A matching manifest proves served build identity, not scientific truth or medical validity.
